content.verdict: The Residence Zanzibar is an elegant five-star retreat on the island's quieter southwest coast, offering 66 private pool villas inspired by traditional Swahili architecture set among swaying palms between two sandy beaches. It strikes an excellent balance between high-end luxury, genuine Zanzibar character, and relative value compared to a private island — making it one of the best full-service honeymoon hotels on the island. Families do visit, so couples seeking a completely adults-only atmosphere may prefer Baraza or Zawadi.
content.best_room: Book the Two-Bedroom Pool Villa for maximum privacy and space — a 396-square-metre sanctuary with a private pool, outdoor rain shower, sunken lounge, and a personal villa host. For a more intimate (and more affordable) stay, the One-Bedroom Pool Villa is equally well-appointed with a private plunge pool, terrace daybed, and handcrafted Zanzibari furniture that feels authentically local without sacrificing any comfort.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Villa Champagne Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Your villa host greets you at reception and escorts you through the fragrant gardens to your private pool villa. A bottle of Champagne chilled beside the pool and fresh tropical flowers scattered on the bed set the tone beautifully. That evening, dine at The Dining Room for an introduction to Zanzibar spice-route cuisine as the Indian Ocean glows under the sunset.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa Morning & Sunset Beach
itinerary_7_nights.description: Begin your day with a couples' ritual at The Spa — their signature Swahili Journey uses locally harvested seaweed, volcanic pumice, and Zanzibar clove oil in a two-hour ceremony that is one of the most memorable spa experiences in East Africa. Spend the afternoon on Kizimkazi beach, the more secluded of the resort's two beaches, with sundowners served directly on the sand.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Dolphin Safari at Kizimkazi
itinerary_7_nights.description: The southwest coast is home to one of Zanzibar's most reliable wild dolphin populations. The resort arranges an early morning dhow to Kizimkazi village where spinner and bottlenose dolphins are frequently encountered in the bay. Return for a late brunch on your villa terrace, then spend the afternoon drifting between your private pool and the ocean.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Stone Town Day Trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: A resort-organized day trip to UNESCO-listed Stone Town reveals the historic heart of Zanzibar — labyrinthine alleys, carved wooden doors, the old slave market, and the spice markets that once made Zanzibar famous. Return in the evening and arrange for a private dinner beside your pool, prepared and served by your villa host.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Snorkeling at Chumbe Island
itinerary_7_nights.description: Charter a boat to Chumbe Island Coral Park, a protected marine sanctuary with remarkable coral gardens and an extraordinary density of reef fish just 12 kilometres from the resort. Pack a picnic from the kitchen and spend the full day in the water. This is arguably the best snorkeling day trip from the southwest coast.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spice Tour & Sunset Dhow Cruise
itinerary_7_nights.description: A guided spice plantation tour in the island's interior introduces you to cloves, nutmeg, vanilla, cardamom, and ylang ylang growing in their natural environment — a genuinely fascinating and aromatic half-day. Return in time for a private sunset dhow cruise from the jetty, with Champagne, chilled prawns, and the Zanzibar coastline glowing behind you.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Last Morning at the Villa
itinerary_7_nights.description: Arrange a late checkout and spend your final morning exactly as you choose — breakfast in bed, one last swim, or a final walk along the quieter south beach as the early light catches the ocean. The Residence's team has a gift for understated, memorable farewells.

caveats: The southwest coast does not have Zanzibar's most photogenic beaches — the water is shallower and occasionally seaweed washes ashore, particularly during certain seasonal periods; the resort rakes it daily but it is not the powder-white north and east coast experience.
Families with children are present at The Residence, which is wonderful for some couples but not ideal for those seeking a purely romantic atmosphere.
The resort is large (66 villas) so it lacks the micro-boutique intimacy of Mnemba or Zawadi — it is genuinely five-star but not exclusively intimate.

faqs.question: Is The Residence Zanzibar good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: The Residence is an excellent honeymoon choice for couples who want five-star service, a private pool villa, and multiple dining and spa options without paying private-island prices. The villa experience is genuinely romantic, the spa is world-class, and the Swahili aesthetic creates a sense of place that generic resorts often miss. It suits couples who want a balance of activity, relaxation, and authentic local culture.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: June through October is the peak season with dry weather, consistent sunshine, and lower humidity — perfect for honeymooners. December through February is also excellent. Avoid April and May (the long rains) when the southwest coast can receive sustained rainfall.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Reserve the couples' spa journey at booking — it is the resort's signature treatment and books up quickly. Also pre-arrange your private pool dinner at least two days ahead, and notify the team of your honeymoon so they can prepare the villa for arrival.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: The Residence Zanzibar is not adults-only. Families with children do visit, particularly during school holidays. The private villa layout means you have plenty of seclusion, but the beach and restaurant areas are shared. Couples seeking a fully adults-only experience should consider Baraza Resort or Zawadi Hotel on the east coast.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: The One-Bedroom Pool Villa is the ideal honeymoon room — private plunge pool, outdoor shower, and a personal villa host who learns your preferences within the first day. Upgrading to the Two-Bedroom Pool Villa adds significantly more space and a larger pool if budget allows.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Zanzibar International Airport (ZNZ), which receives direct flights from Nairobi, Dar es Salaam, Addis Ababa, and several European cities seasonally. The resort is approximately 45 minutes south of the airport by road; the hotel arranges transfers that include a welcome drink on arrival.
